metadata.title:
|THE TRUTH ABOUT GIRLS Who WORK OVERTIME"

What Prosperity Means to the Girls in the Mills and
Factories Ann Adams Tells of the Weary Procession
of Leaden Minutes That Make Up the Extra Two Hours
Entailed by Good Times

pages.raw_text:
more money working only 10. But
our jobs


Liz stopped for moment to put
her finger in her mouth to such the
blood from a nasty cut made by a
steel shaving. The girls hands were
SCARED and scratched. Her fingers
were Calloused. She said she did
not notice steel splinters working
into the flesh of her hands. but
did. For days after ! left the shop,
spent my spare time picking liltle
needle-like points of steel out of my
hands.


Twice a day man came to our
bin to weigh our completed work.
Ont of curiosity, | asked him how
many pounds we had sorted. He
did not answer


How many pounds can you sort
in day." | questioned Liz.


Il don't know" she answered.
They never tell us how much work
Ne do. We just get our pay envel-
obes."


"All the girls get about the same,
don't they"


Just about" said the best sorter
in the whole factory.


Doesn't make much difference
whether you work fast or slow?'


Except that you get called down
if you go SLOW" said Liz. They
find out how fast you can work, and
you have to keep up your speed
Some girls cant work only just so
fast"


But if YOU do three times as
much work in a week as the girl
! ext to you. why don't you get three
times as much money!"


Say, look here" scolded Liz, Il
don't know. You'll never get as
much as she does if you don't talk
less and work faster"


II was only little after a O'Clock
of an April day, but the shop was
so dark that Liz switched on the
electric light over our bin. Each
bin had its drop light. The shop
was equipped for working hours to
extend both before and after day
light.


I'll be glad when it's warm
enough to have the windows up
remarked Liz, for then we can look
out at the city and the clouds. !
love to watch the clouds"


] glanced up at the window which
We faced. The glass was Opaque
from dirt.


II like to wonder what is out
there beyond the place where I
can't see Liz continued dreamily.
Il suppose there are lots of fields
and flowers and things. I've been
to the parks sometimes on Sundays.
and ! suppose it is maybe as pretty
as the parks out there only more
of it. | have never been outside the
city, you know.


Didn't you ever have a vaca-
tion?" | asked.


NO" answered the girl who had
lived through IS summers, Il have
never had a vacation. But this sum-
mer | am going on a Sunday eXcur-
sion. 1t costs $1.75 round trip
